themes.exampleQuote: I always make sure to try their new flavors because it always surprises me
the portion of these two standards is mind boggling... never received a portion so small for $7
I loved the waffle cones which they make fresh throughout the day
That gooey butter cake ice cream has me in a chokehold
It was stretched out further than a Utah wild wild west, but we stuck it out

notable_quote: I walked in there forgetful as a June bug, and I walked out feeling brand new.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers reliably light up over the rotating flavor program and freshly made waffle cones, but a recurring undercurrent of sticker shock — specifically the scoop-to-dollar ratio — keeps the sentiment ceiling below what the enthusiasm for the ice cream itself would otherwise support.
quarter_narrative: Twenty-nine Pittsburgh creators filmed Jeni's this quarter and said nothing back to them about it. Those 30-plus TikToks stacked up 147,000 plays on the strength of one flavor — gooey butter cake — becoming a localized word-of-mouth engine that Jeni's national account, and its absent local TikTok presence, had nothing to do with. Meanwhile in the Pittsburgh Sweets family, Page's is the leader this issue precisely because of social momentum; Jeni's is sitting at #3 despite having a deeper creator footprint than almost any shop in the category. What closes the gap between #3 and the top of the family is whether that TikTok energy ever gets a response from the business itself — not to recapture it, but to stop leaving it entirely on the table.
tldr_read: Strong creator coverage and a perfect conversion path, but review sentiment sits below the family median and the business has no TikTok presence despite being the most-filmed shop in the category — Icons tier, #2 in Pittsburgh Ice Cream.
tldr_meaning: The rank holds on the strength of creator volume and conversion infrastructure, but sentiment is the soft ceiling: until the portion-value friction stops appearing in reviews, the gap to #1 in ice cream and the top of Pittsburgh Sweets stays structural.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Gooey Butter Cake Built a Following. Jeni's Didn't Have to Do a Thing — and That's the Problem.

playbook.headline: Respond to the portion-value friction directly
playbook.action: Two reviews explicitly call out scoop size for $7; acknowledge portion standards in-store signage or update menu descriptions to set expectations before purchase.

playbook.headline: Claim a TikTok presence before the next quarter
playbook.action: Twenty-nine creators have already done the work — 147,532 plays with zero TikTok posts from the business itself; even a repost account closes that gap meaningfully.

playbook.headline: Close the eight-day Instagram gap
playbook.action: With 12 posts and 4 reels in 30 days the cadence is reasonable, but the 0.49% engagement rate suggests content isn't landing — tie posts to specific flavor drops rather than general product shots.
